referenced_message.author: jamalski_
referenced_message.content:
I understand the distinction you're trying to make. What I'm not convinced of is the idea that reducing a skill's radius, effective reach, reliability, and overall success rate somehow stops being a nerf because we're calling it a utility adjustment instead.
Before suggesting that others need to think more critically, it might be worth considering the cumulative impact of the changes being proposed rather than evaluating each one in isolation.

I understand the distinction you're trying to make. What I'm not convinced of is the idea that reducing a skill's radius, effective reach, reliability, and overall success rate somehow stops being a nerf because we're calling it a utility adjustment instead.

referenced_message.content: That’s incorrect. The radius is so large you can not out run it or dodge roll to avoid it. The downtime is so short that you can be killed fully respawn in conflict with them camping the spawn and you can shock as soon as their permashield is gone again. The run up shock mechanic should have a slightly larger radius than throwing the shock since there is an animation that makes you vulnerable in that moment. You can throw the shock also behind the shield which is overpowered and you can throw it to far. With the current radius it’s completely unbalanced. Reducing the radius on that would effectively require more skill, but afford the opposing player a greater chance of avoiding. These aren’t major nerfs brother, you just can’t cope that it’s broken. This makes 100% sense in balancing the skill by creating a balanced statistic of success and failure while keeping the actual shock mechanic in function. You don’t have to change the function to balance you can simply change the mechanics behind it and skill gap.

referenced_message.author: jamalski_
referenced_message.content:
You're changing the argument though. First you suggested reducing the radius, reducing the throw range, and increasing the cooldown to 45 seconds. That's what I replied to.

Now you're arguing about counterplay, which is a different discussion entirely.

If the issue is that the stun interaction has too little counterplay, then let's discuss that. But reducing the radius, reducing the effective range, and putting it on a 45 second cooldown doesn't create counterplay, it just makes the skill bad.

Balance should be about finding reasonable tradeoffs, not stacking multiple major nerfs onto the same ability and calling it balanced.

content: If the developers can adjust the skill separately for PvP without affecting its PvE performance, then I'm 100% in favor of that. My concern has always been about changes that end up hurting the PvE experience.

referenced_message.author: jamalski_
referenced_message.content:
I run a lot of Legendary content and not every run goes according to a script, especially when helping less optimized players. There have been plenty of times where the stun helped save teammates or recover a bad situation.

My concern is that some of these suggested changes seem more likely to make the skill significantly weaker overall rather than simply balance it.

referenced_message.author: jamalski_
referenced_message.content:
The problem is that you're looking at the skill from a PvP only perspective. A 2 to 3 second charge up might sound reasonable in the Dark Zone, but it would significantly hurt the skill in PvE where it's used for crowd control, threat generation, and stopping enemy pushes.

Personally, I'm a much bigger fan of PvE than PvP, so when I look at balance changes, I also consider how they affect Legendary missions, group play, and end game content. The game has to balance both PvP and PvE. A change that feels good for one mode can easily make the skill feel clunky or unreliable in the other.

That's why I'm generally more in favor of PvP specific adjustments rather than changing a core skill in a way that affects the entire class everywhere.

The game has to balance both PvP and PvE. A change that feels good for one mode can easily make the skill feel clunky or unreliable in the other.

That's why I'm generally more in favor of PvP specific adjustments rather than changing a core skill in a way that affects the entire class everywhere.

Spike radius is way too wide should be a fixed radius that allows players to dodge roll at the right time to avoid. Throwing the spike should be an even smaller radius of effect. The time for the skill to refresh is way to quick as well and should be a fixed time of 45 sec or something

That's fair, but it actually supports my point. If players believe Vanguard clears are overtuned and some Juggernaut builds are overtuned, then the discussion should be about specific builds, scaling interactions, or mechanics rather than claiming an entire class is broken.

A class performing exceptionally well at the highest level of optimization doesn't automatically mean the class itself is overpowered. The same way not every Vanguard is clearing content in five minutes, not every Juggernaut is running around unkillable. Balance discussions should focus on outlier builds and interactions, not on deleting a class's core identity.
no one finds vanguard five min clears balanced, in fact people actively want it to be nerfed

referenced_message.author: jamalski_
referenced_message.content:
I find it interesting that when a Vanguard clears a Legendary dungeon in five minutes through extremely high damage output, most players consider that balanced because it is a DPS class. However, when a Juggernaut survives heavy incoming damage and performs the role it was specifically designed for, some players immediately label it as overpowered.

A DPS class should excel at damage, and a tank class should excel at survivability. The real balance concern is not whether a Juggernaut can survive, but whether it can survive while also matching the clear speed and damage output of dedicated DPS classes. If Vanguard can clear content significantly faster while Juggernaut survives significantly longer, then both classes are simply fulfilling their intended roles.

Survivability on a tank should not be treated differently than damage on a DPS. If one is considered acceptable because it aligns with the class fantasy, then the other should be viewed the same way.

content:
People see a strong Juggernaut and immediately blame the class instead of the build. A properly built Field Medic can outheal massive damage and kill players in the Dark Zone, and a properly built Aegis can be incredibly difficult to bring down as well.

There was even a video of a Field Medic surviving and killing a blatant Bulwark hacker in the Dark Zone, yet nobody was asking for Field Medic nerfs afterward.

Every class becomes powerful when heavily invested into and optimized correctly. People accept the damage output of a fully optimized DPS build, but when a fully optimized tank build excels at survivability, it suddenly becomes a balance issue. The issue is that people often compare a fully optimized Juggernaut to average builds from other classes and then conclude that the class itself is overpowered. If survivability alone is considered broken, then by that logic Aegis and Field Medic should be receiving the same complaints.

In Dark Zone the bulwarks are a way to strong.

Every time you loot a boss chest *(even if you do not steal it)*, you know to 100% the next of the boy with the shield.

rogue, stun, ulti, dead

I find it interesting that when a Vanguard clears a Legendary dungeon in five minutes through extremely high damage output, most players consider that balanced because it is a DPS class. However, when a Juggernaut survives heavy incoming damage and performs the role it was specifically designed for, some players immediately label it as overpowered.

A DPS class should excel at damage, and a tank class should excel at survivability. The real balance concern is not whether a Juggernaut can survive, but whether it can survive while also matching the clear speed and damage output of dedicated DPS classes. If Vanguard can clear content significantly faster while Juggernaut survives significantly longer, then both classes are simply fulfilling their intended roles.

Survivability on a tank should not be treated differently than damage on a DPS. If one is considered acceptable because it aligns with the class fantasy, then the other should be viewed the same way.

Juggernaut is intended to be a tank, it should be difficult to kill. The real question is whether its damage output is too high relative to its survivability, not whether it survives longer than other classes.
This is amazing and 100% correct

referenced_message.author: gianlucag._
referenced_message.content:
Tank Bulwark needs improvements to its aggro generation. Many times, even when using the proper aggro skill mods, I activate my abilities and a large number of enemies simply ignore them.

The range and amount of aggro that can be pulled should also be improved. It shouldn't be limited to only enemies that are close to the agent. In this game, especially in Elite missions, enemies deal a lot of damage, and having a tank that can only pull aggro from nearby targets makes the experience unnecessarily frustrating for both DPS players and the tank itself.

I believe this should be changed so that all enemies in the room immediately focus on the Bulwark when aggro-generating abilities are used. If a player chooses to build and play as a tank, those abilities should reliably fulfill their intended role.

Stun is broken completely being able to re pick up the stun is insane. Combine that with the Pummel OS that stuns.... Its imossible to outplay. Last the damage from the ult SHOULDN'T under any reason be so high its comparable to a vanguard tac link ult. That ult does way too much damage when specced correctly... My bad one more thing the ult also shouldn't stun if the damage remains that high. Stuns in PVP absolutely need to break on damage taken. Can build way too tanky making it unkillable(least of my worries stuns and damage from ult is way more concerning).

Couldn’t hurt. It’s the giant AoE and inability to resist or counter the stun that’s annoying. There should be some counterplay whether it’s a stat to build into to resist, a skill that helps counter the AoE, or a break free button combination or similar to break out of the stun early if you have good reflexes. Or make it dodgeable or something.
